Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows significantly elevated serum creatinine, indicating impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals marked proteinuria, glycosuria, and ketonuria, with low specific gravity and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal). These findings suggest possible diabetic nephropathy or another form of renal dysfunction, with no evidence of urinary tract infection. Further nephrological evaluation and assessment for diabetes are recommended.
